text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer. The most important aspect is making your code readable, so that others—and your future self—can easily understand it. Use clear naming conventions: variable names should describe their purpose, like `customerName` instead of `cn`. Functions should do one thing only and have a descriptive name, such as `calculateTotalPrice()`. This makes the code modular and easier to test. Good practices like these improve maintainability, meaning you can fix bugs or add features without breaking everything else. In short, clean code saves time and reduces frustration.
